title: Agent identity in Claude Tag: a new access model for autonomous, team-wide AI
preview: For an AI agent to do its best work on a human-agent team, it needs access to the same tools, documents, and context humans have.

blocks.english: For an AI agent to do its best work on a human-agent team, it needs access to the same tools, documents, and context humans have.
blocks.chinese: 要让 AI 智能体在人类与智能体协作的团队中发挥最佳作用，它需要能够访问人类所使用的相同工具、文档和上下文。

blocks.english: In a "single player" AI experience (where one person chats with one assistant), that's straightforward: you connect your own accounts and the agent acts on your behalf. But in a "multiplayer" AI experience like Claude Tag, Claude sits in a shared channel alongside many people at once, and it draws on the tools and context that belong to the workspace, rather than any one individual.
blocks.chinese: 在"单人" AI 体验中（一个人与一个助手对话），这很简单：你连接自己的账户，智能体代表你行动。但在像 Claude Tag 这样的"多人" AI 体验中，Claude 同时位于一个共享频道中，与多人在一起，它使用的是属于工作区的工具和上下文，而不是任何个人的。

blocks.english: To make multiplayer experiences work, Claude needs its own accounts for those tools, set up by an admin and tied to the workspace. We call this access model agent identity.
blocks.chinese: 为了让多人体验正常工作，Claude 需要拥有自己的工具账户，由管理员设置并与工作区绑定。我们将这种访问模型称为智能体身份。

blocks.english: In this post, we explain how agent identity works, how it moves permissions from per-user to per-channel, and how to scope it well in your own workspace.
blocks.chinese: 在这篇文章中，我们将解释智能体身份如何工作，它如何将权限从按用户分配转变为按频道分配，以及如何在你的工作区中合理设定其范围。

blocks.english: Why "act as the user" breaks down
blocks.chinese: 为什么"代表用户行动"模式会失效

blocks.english: When you use AI as a personal assistant, you can connect platforms like Google Drive, GitHub, and your calendar, and let the model use your access permissions to read and write in them.
blocks.chinese: 当你将 AI 用作个人助理时，你可以连接 Google Drive、GitHub 和日历等平台，让模型使用你的访问权限在这些平台上进行读写操作。

blocks.english: This model doesn't work for Claude Tag for two reasons:
blocks.chinese: 这种模式在 Claude Tag 中行不通，原因有两个：

blocks.english: Increasing agent autonomy. The length of a task that an AI agent can reliably complete on its own has been doubling roughly every four months. Agents now schedule their own tasks for later and respond to events long after the person who asked has logged off. While users set up routines that trigger them to act given certain situations, the agent works largely autonomously.
blocks.chinese: 智能体自主性不断提升。AI 智能体能够自主可靠完成的任务时长大约每四个月翻一番。智能体现在可以为自己安排后续任务，并在用户下线很长时间后响应事件。虽然用户设置了触发智能体在特定情况下行动的例程，但智能体在很大程度上是自主工作的。

blocks.english: Multiplayer teams. Claude Tag places Claude in shared spaces where teams are already working—e.g., a channel where three engineers and a PM are debugging together. But when more than one person is steering, whose permissions apply? There's no single choice of person that'd be right all of the time. This gives admins the ability to define what an agent can do in Slack independent from the humans involved, and a distinct tracking of what is done in Slack.
blocks.chinese: 多人协作团队。Claude Tag 将 Claude 放置在团队已经正在工作的共享空间中——例如，一个三位工程师和一位产品经理正在共同调试的频道。但当多人在指导时，应该使用谁的权限？没有哪一个单一的人选始终是正确的。这使管理员能够独立于相关人员定义智能体在 Slack 中可以做什么，并对在 Slack 中的操作进行清晰的追踪。

blocks.english: Claude acts as itself
blocks.chinese: Claude 以自身身份行动

blocks.english: In a channel where Claude Tag is active, Claude isn't acting on behalf of a single user. It has its own account in each system it touches: it posts in Slack as the Claude app, opens pull requests as the Claude GitHub App, and queries your warehouse under a service account provisioned by an admin.
blocks.chinese: 在 Claude Tag 活跃的频道中，Claude 不是代表单个用户行动。它在所接触的每个系统中都有自己的账户：它以 Claude 应用的身份在 Slack 中发帖，以 Claude GitHub 应用的身份打开拉取请求，并使用管理员配置的服务账户查询你的数据仓库。

blocks.english: And because there are no personal user credentials in play, a shared channel can never become a side door into someone's private documents.
blocks.chinese: 而且由于没有个人用户凭证参与其中，共享频道永远不会成为访问他人私人文档的后门。

blocks.english: Inheriting permissions
blocks.chinese: 继承权限

blocks.english: In the agent identity model, admins define an identity—the baseline set of connections and skills Claude holds everywhere—at the workspace level, and every channel inherits it by default. Then, where it makes sense, they can override it at the channel level, such as by granting the engineering channel access to GitHub and the data warehouse, or confining a CRM connection to a single private channel.
blocks.chinese: 在智能体身份模型中，管理员在工作区级别定义一个身份——Claude 在任何地方都拥有的基线连接和技能集——每个频道默认继承该身份。然后，在合理的情况下，他们可以在频道级别覆盖这些设置，例如授予工程频道访问 GitHub 和数据仓库的权限，或者将 CRM 连接限制在单个私有频道中。

blocks.english: In addition to credentials, admins also define:
blocks.chinese: 除了凭证之外，管理员还需要定义：

blocks.english: Repository access: which repos Claude can read and write to. Connectors: the tools and API keys that Claude uses to do its job. Across an organization, different API keys can connect to the same service at different permission levels (e.g., Claude might be given read-only warehouse access in a general channel, and write access in the data team's private one). Skills and plugins: folders of instructions, scripts, and resources Claude loads dynamically to improve performance on specialized tasks. Standing instructions: custom instructions and context for each channel.
blocks.chinese: 仓库访问权限：Claude 可以读写哪些代码仓库。连接器：Claude 用来完成工作的工具和 API 密钥。在整个组织中，不同的 API 密钥可以以不同的权限级别连接到同一服务（例如，在通用频道中 Claude 可能拥有只读仓库访问权限，而在数据团队的私有频道中则拥有写入权限）。技能和插件：指令、脚本和资源的文件夹，Claude 会动态加载这些内容以提高在专业任务上的表现。常设指令：每个频道的自定义指令和上下文。

blocks.english: Because this model works around distinct Claude identities, revoking the identity ends Claude's access everywhere that the identity was used. This takes much less effort to manage than auditing individual agent actions across dozens of user accounts.
blocks.chinese: 由于此模型围绕不同的 Claude 身份工作，撤销该身份将终止 Claude 在所有使用该身份的地方的访问权限。这比在数十个用户账户中审计单个智能体操作要容易管理得多。

blocks.english: How the agent identity model works
blocks.chinese: 智能体身份模型如何工作

blocks.english: Agent identity replaces the question "what can this user do?" with "what can this agent do in this compartment?" That's a departure from per-user Access Control Lists: it means that a channel member without direct access to the repo can ask Claude to read that repo, if the channel's profile grants Claude that permission.
blocks.chinese: 智能体身份将"这个用户能做什么？"的问题替换为"这个智能体在这个分区中能做什么？"这与按用户的访问控制列表不同：这意味着，如果频道的配置文件授予 Claude 权限，那么没有直接仓库访问权限的频道成员可以请 Claude 读取该仓库。

blocks.english: This is unusual, but we think it is a necessary step toward an access model that works for autonomous, multiplayer agents. Below, we sketch out how to think about setting those boundaries.
blocks.chinese: 这不同寻常，但我们认为这是迈向适用于自主、多人智能体的访问模型的必要一步。下面，我们概述了如何思考设定这些边界。

blocks.english: How identity boundaries work
blocks.chinese: 身份边界如何工作

blocks.english: Claude Tag creates a distinct identity for each private channel; public channels in a workspace share a workspace-level identity. Claude's identity in a legal channel can't reach code that wasn't granted there, and its identity in an engineering channel can't read legal documents that weren't granted there. Memory and access respect those boundaries: what Claude learns in a private channel never appears in the wider workspace.
blocks.chinese: Claude Tag 为每个私有频道创建一个独立的身份；工作区中的公共频道共享一个工作区级别的身份。Claude 在法律频道中的身份无法访问未被授予权限的代码，其在工程频道中的身份也无法读取未被授予权限的法律文档。记忆和访问都遵循这些边界：Claude 在私有频道中学到的内容永远不会出现在更广泛的工作区中。

blocks.english: The identity belongs to the channel, so anyone in it can tag Claude by default, and admins can scope each channel's profile to the least-privileged member. On Enterprise plans, role-based access control lets admins go further and decide which members can invoke Claude at all, so a channel governs both what the agent can reach and who can ask.
blocks.chinese: 身份属于频道，因此默认情况下频道中的任何人都可以提及 Claude，管理员可以将每个频道的配置文件范围限定为权限最低的成员。在企业版套餐中，基于角色的访问控制让管理员可以更进一步，决定哪些成员可以调用 Claude，因此频道既控制智能体可以访问什么，也控制谁可以提出请求。

blocks.english: Broad default access to tools and context
blocks.chinese: 广泛的工具和上下文默认访问

blocks.alt: Claude Tag 中工具访问范围的划分方式：低风险的集成在共享频道中以智能体身份运行，个人或团队专属工具保留在私信中以用户身份运行

blocks.english: Running Claude Tag inside Anthropic, we found that its value compounds with tool and context access. Each connected system makes every other one more useful, because Claude can combine context across them—pulling a thread from Slack, a doc from Drive, a ticket from a tracker, and a query from a warehouse into one answer that no single tool could provide.
blocks.chinese: 在 Anthropic 内部运行 Claude Tag 时，我们发现其价值随着工具和上下文访问的增加而倍增。每个连接的系统都使其他系统更有用，因为 Claude 可以跨系统组合上下文——从 Slack 中提取讨论串，从 Drive 中获取文档，从追踪器中获取工单，从数据仓库中获取查询——整合成一个单一工具无法提供的答案。

blocks.english: The teams that get the most out of Claude are the ones that grant it generous access from the start, and pare access back depending on their organization's admin preferences. Agent identity gives admins broad enough scope for Claude to do useful cross-system work, with boundaries firm enough that the access never travels somewhere it wasn't granted. Our advice is to start with a baseline profile in a few channels, read the audit trail, and then extend access where the work justifies it, one deliberate grant at a time.
blocks.chinese: 从 Claude 中获益最多的团队，是那些从一开始就授予其充足访问权限的团队，然后根据组织的管理员偏好逐步缩减权限。智能体身份为管理员提供了足够广泛的范围，让 Claude 能够执行有用的跨系统工作，同时边界足够牢固，确保访问权限不会扩散到未被授予的地方。我们的建议是，先在几个频道中建立基线配置文件，查看审计记录，然后根据工作需要逐步扩展访问权限，每次有意识地授予一个权限。

blocks.english: For organizations that require even more granularity, admins can disable Claude Tag in specific channels. Admins can also apply role-based access controls (RBAC) to limit access to Claude Tag to specific users.
blocks.chinese: 对于需要更细粒度的组织，管理员可以在特定频道中禁用 Claude Tag。管理员还可以应用基于角色的访问控制（RBAC），将 Claude Tag 的访问权限限制在特定用户。

blocks.english: Direct messages
blocks.chinese: 私信

blocks.english: With Claude Tag, direct messages work differently than in shared channels. DMs run on users' individual claude.ai accounts—their connectors, credentials, and name on the result. This makes DMs the right place to work with Claude on tasks and with tools that should never live in a channel, like email drafts or software only you have a license for.
blocks.chinese: 在 Claude Tag 中，私信的工作方式与共享频道不同。私信在用户个人的 claude.ai 账户下运行——使用他们自己的连接器、凭证，并在结果上显示他们的名字。这使得私信成为与 Claude 合作处理那些永远不应该出现在频道中的任务和工具的合适场所，比如邮件草稿或只有你拥有许可的软件。

blocks.english: Security and audit
blocks.chinese: 安全与审计

blocks.english: When an admin adds a connection to a channel's profile, the credential is stored independently and mapped to that channel's identity, then injected at the network boundary at request time. Outbound traffic to any host an admin hasn't allowed is blocked outright. On the audit side, every routine, memory write, and network call made with agent credentials is recorded, and because Claude acts under its own service accounts, those actions also land in each connected system's own logs.
blocks.chinese: 当管理员向频道的配置文件添加连接时，凭证将独立存储并映射到该频道的身份，然后在请求时在网络边界注入。发送到管理员未允许的任何主机的出站流量将被直接阻止。在审计方面，使用智能体凭证进行的每个例程、内存写入和网络调用都会被记录，并且由于 Claude 以自己的服务账户行动，这些操作也会出现在每个连接系统的各自日志中。

blocks.english: What's next
blocks.chinese: 未来展望

blocks.english: Agent identity is the foundation of Claude Tag's access model. In the future, we plan to strengthen our Claude Tag's security offerings to include just-in-time credential grants—so that a user can approve a single sensitive action in the moment without permanently widening the agent's scope—and an identity-aware overlay for organizations with more complex clearance structures. This will add user-level checks on top of an agent's scope, so Claude only acts when both the channel's profile and the requesting user's own permissions allow it.
blocks.chinese: 智能体身份是 Claude Tag 访问模型的基础。未来，我们计划加强 Claude Tag 的安全功能，包括即时凭证授予——这样用户可以即时批准单个敏感操作，而无需永久扩大智能体的范围——以及为具有更复杂权限结构的组织提供身份感知覆盖层。这将在智能体范围之上增加用户级检查，因此 Claude 仅在频道的配置文件和请求用户自身的权限都允许时才会行动。

blocks.english: The shift from single player to multiplayer AI in products like Claude Tag makes long-running, team-based work possible. Agent identity ensures that Claude's access to tools is broad enough to be useful, but scoped enough to be secure at enterprise scale.
blocks.chinese: 从单人 AI 到多人 AI 的转变，在 Claude Tag 这样的产品中使长期、基于团队的协作成为可能。智能体身份确保 Claude 对工具的访问权限足够广泛以发挥作用，但范围又足够限定，以便在企业规模下保持安全。

blocks.text: This article was written by Noah Zweben, a member of technical staff on the Claude Code team.
